title: The Stone-Archives Where Water Writes

features.name: the-oldest-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Stone cups that held the same water for ten thousand years. You feel the smoothness where current learned its argument.

features.name: sediment-layers
features.desc-on-touch: Each stratum a year the water could not speak aloud. You read the archive from bottom to top: drought, flood, drought, patience.

features.name: carved-cipher
features.desc-on-touch: The river wrote its own language into stone long before the archive. You trace the glyphs and find they mean: I am still here.

features.name: the-reading-shelf
features.desc-on-touch: A ledge where the current slows enough to leave its messages. Stone memorized the weight of water; you memorize the weight of time.

features.name: the-throat-stone
features.desc-on-touch: Where the channel narrows and the water learns to speak. All the river's memory concentrates here; you feel it amplified.

inscription: the river wrote before words; the stone listened and learned to remember in its own dialect

file.name: Water_Stone_Dialogue_Fragment_Quaternary.txt
file.note: Margin notes by hand, some water-stained: the archive did not build itself; the water built us; we are only the stone the water taught to keep.

exits.label: back toward the channel's mouth, where the archive learned to cup water
deeper into bedrock, where the river's first voice still echoes
upstream, where tributaries remember being separate
downstream into sediment, where meaning dissolves into silt
